back_translation: Therefore, rid yourselves of all malice, all deceit, hypocrisy, envy, and all kinds of slander
Like newborn babies, crave the pure spiritual milk free from deceit, so that you may grow up by it toward salvation
since you have tasted that the Lord is excellent
When you come to Him, who is the living stone, rejected by men but in God’s sight chosen and precious
you also, like living stones, are being built into a spiritual temple, to be a holy priesthood, to offer spiritual sacrifices that please God, through Jesus Christ
For there is a saying in Scripture, “Behold, I lay in Zion a stone, a chosen and precious cornerstone, and the one who believes in Him will not be put to shame at all”
Therefore, for you who believe, He is precious. But for those who do not believe, “The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone”
and, “A stone of stumbling and a rock that causes offense.” They stumble because they do not obey the word, to which they have been appointed

But you all are “a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for His own possession,” so that you may proclaim the excellencies of Him who called you out of darkness into His marvelous light
Once you were not a people, but now you are the people of God; once you had not received mercy, but now you have received mercy
Beloved, as foreigners and temporary residents, I urge you to abstain from desires of the flesh which wage war against the soul
Conduct yourselves well among the Gentiles, so that in matters where they slander you as evildoers, they may see your good works and glorify God on the day He visits us
For the Lord’s sake, submit to every human institution, whether to a king as the supreme authority
or to governors as those sent by the king to punish wrongdoers and praise those who do good
For this is the will of God, that by doing good you may silence the ignorance of foolish people
Live as free people, but do not use your freedom as a covering for evil, but live as servants of God
Honor everyone, love the brotherhood, fear God, honor the king
Servants, submit yourselves to your masters with all reverence, not only to the good and gentle ones, but even to the unreasonable ones
For it is grace that pleases God, if anyone endures pains in unjust suffering for the sake of conscience toward God
For what credit is it if you endure being beaten because of sin? But if you endure suffering for doing good, this is grace that pleases God
For to this you were called, because Christ also suffered for you, leaving you an example so that you might walk in His footsteps
“He committed no sin, and no deceit was found in His mouth”
When He was reviled, He did not revile in return; when He suffered, He did not threaten anyone, but entrusted Himself to the One who judges righteously
He Himself bore our sins in His body on the tree, so that we might die to sin and live for righteousness. “By His wounds you have been healed”
For “you used to be like sheep going astray,” but now you have returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls
